message:
🐛 Fix 26% coverage test failure rate from ESM live-binding breakage (#10474)

* 🐛 Fix flaky tests from clusterCache reference breakage (Fixes #10460)

Use Object.assign to preserve clusterCache reference identity
instead of reassigning, preventing subscriber stale-reference bugs
that caused intermittent test failures.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix 26% coverage test failure rate from ESM live-binding breakage (Fixes #10460)

The vitest CJS transform does not preserve ESM live bindings for `let`
exports. When shared.ts reassigned `clusterCache = { ... }`, test files
holding an imported reference to the old object saw stale data (empty
clusters array), causing ~70 tests to fail across 5 test files.

Root causes fixed:
- shared.ts: Replace all `clusterCache = { ... }` reassignments with
  `Object.assign(clusterCache, { ... })` to preserve object identity
- shared.ts: Add `getHealthCheckFailures()` and `getInitialFetchStarted()`
  getter functions for module-level `let` variables that tests need to read
- shared-coverage.test.ts: Restore real agentFetch via `vi.importActual`
  instead of inheriting the global setup mock (which replaced agentFetch
  with a passthrough, breaking token-injection tests)
- shared-pure-funcs.test.ts, shared-coverage.test.ts: Pre-seed
  `kc-agent-token` in localStorage before fetchWithRetry tests so
  agentFetch does not call fetch('/api/agent/token'), which doubled
  the fetch call count and broke call-count assertions
- shared-cache-fetch.test.ts, shared-websocket-detect.test.ts: Use
  getter functions instead of direct `let` variable reads

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
